properties.remark: THE PUBLIC REPORTED QUARTER TO GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL 9 MILES WEST OF WAMSUTTER ALONG INTERSTATE 80 ALONG WITH AN ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUST.

WAMSUTTER POLICE REPORTED POSSIBLE POWER LINES DOWN IN TOWN. PUBLIC ALSO REPORTED A SEMI WAS ROLLED OVER BY THE WIND AND THAT SIDING WAS TORE OFF OF HOMES IN THE AREA. THIS SPOTTER ALSO REPORTED NUMEROUS BROKEN WINDOWS.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONCLUDED EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED AT A RANCH EAST OF MANDERSON. TORNADO PATH LENGTH ABOUT ONE-HALF MILE. TORNADO WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 35 YARDS. RANCH HOME TOOK DIRECT HIT WITH THREE OCCUPANTS INSIDE.
HAIL FELL FOR 15 MINUTES.
ONE LARGE TREE DOWN BLOCKING THE ROAD ADJACENT TO THE LIBRARY. NEWSPAPER STANDS BLOWN OVER.
BRANCHES BLOWN OFF TREES.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN BEAVER CREEK.
BRANCHES BLOWN OFF TREES WITH HEAVY RAIN.
TREES DOWN IN SHELL CANYON ON HIGHWAY 14 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 20 AND 21.
ESTIMATED 55 TO 65 MPH WINDS.
VERY HEAVY RAIN
DEPUTY SHERIFF REPORTED TORNADO ON GROUND FOR 3 MINUTES AND DIME SIZE HAIL SOUTHEAST OF HAMILTON DOME AREA.
TREE FELL ON CAR AND SHATTERED WIND SHIELD.
POWER LINES AND SMALL TREE LIMBS DOWN.
TREE FELL ON CAR ON DEATH CANYON ROAD. NO INJURIES. EXTENT OF CAR DAMAGE UNKNOWN.
ONE INCH HAIL.
PUBLIC REPORT VIA THERMOPOLIS POLICE DEPT. MARBLE TO ONE INCH SIZED HAIL.
THERMOPOLIS POLICE REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN TOWN.
TWO HOUSES HAD MAJOR FLOODING. RESIDENCES EVACUATED.
PUBLIC REPORT OF QUARTER SIZED HAIL IN TOWN.
WATER FROM NEARBY OWL CREEK WASHED AWAY HAY FROM A FIELD. WATER WAS OVER WAIST DEEP.
OWL CREEK CAMP GROUND NEAR HIDDEN VALLEY WAS FLOODED.
STRANDED VEHICLES IN DOWNTOWN CASPER WITH FOOT DEEP WATER ON NUMEROUS ROADS. ONE TO ONE AND A HALF INCHES OF RAIN FELL IN ABOUT 30 MINUTES.
*** 1 INJ *** PERSON INJURED DUE TO STORM DRAIN COVER BEING DISPLACED BY SURGING WATER. MULTIPLE HIGH WATER RESCUES. SEVERAL BUSINESSES AND HOMES FLOODED.
A SEMI TRUCK WAS BLOWN OVER BY THE WIND ON INTERSTATE 90 NEAR MILE MARKER 44.
A SEMI TRUCK WAS BLOWN OVER BY THE WIND ON INTERSTATE 90 NEAR MILE MARKER 44.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN.

JOHNSON COUNTY SO REPORTED FLOODING OF AREA RAVINES AND FLOODING DOWNSTREAM FROM ROBINSON CANYON. A CULVERT DOWNSTREAM OF POISON CREEK AND THE MIDDLE FORK CRAZY WOMAN CREEK WERE REPORTED TOTALLY CLOGGED WITH DEBRIS AND THE DEPUTY WAS AWAITING A COUNTY CREW TO CLEAN IT.
HAIL FELL AT MILE MARKER 206 ON INTERSTATE 25.
HAIL OCCURRED BETWEEN 840 AND 852 PM MDT.
COTTONWOOD TREES 3 FEET IN DIAMETER UPROOTED.
SEVERAL OLD COTTONWOOD TREES REPORTED DOWN IN NORTHERN HALF OF HOT SPRINGS STATE PARK.
COTTONWOOD TREES APPROX. 60 FT. TALL REPORTED BLOWN DOWN IN COMMUNITY OF SADDLESTRING.
ACCUMULATIONS OF 1 INCH HAIL REPORTED IN COMMUNITY OF SADDLESTRING.
ESTIMATED 50 TO 60 MPH WINDS.
HAIL OF ONE-HALF TO ONE INCH IN DIAMETER FELL FOR ABOUT FOUR MINUTES.
THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW PEAK WIND MEASURED AT LANDER AIRPORT.
FORT WASHAKIE POLICE REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL ON TROUT CREEK ROAD
HAIL RANGING FROM PEA TO QUARTER SIZE WAS REPORTED NEAR RED CANYON.
DIME TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL FELL AT A RANCH NEAR RED CANYON BETWEEN 545 AND 600 PM.
THE CHIEF JOSEPH WYDOT SENSOR REPORTED A PEAK WIND GUST TO 64 MPH AT 1730L. A NEARBY SENSOR AT CRANDALL REPORTED WIND GUSTS OVER 50 MPH AND OVER A HALF INCH OR RAIN.
HAIL LASTED 5 MINUTES. MOST OF THE HAIL WAS ONE TO TWO INCHES IN DIAMETER.
HAIL SIZE WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AT LEAST TWO INCHES IN DIAMETER WHEN IT LANDED ON AND DENTED THE HOOD OF A TAVELING VEHICLE. MOST OF THE HAIL WAS HALF DOLLAR SIZE.
HAIL BROKE A WINDOW IN A VEHICLE
HAIL BROKE A WINDOW IN A VEHICLE
NICKEL TO GOLF BALL HAIL FELL AT THE POWDER RIVER REST AREA FOR SEVERAL MINUTES.
LARGEST HAIL STONE WAS 1 1/8 INCH. HAIL LASTED FOR SEVERAL MINUTES.
68 MPH WIND GUST AT 630 PM MDT REPORTED BY CASPER AIRPORT ASOS.
1 INCH HAIL REPORTED.
